segments.text: The Lord told Moses and Aaron,
Give these instructions to the Israelites. These are the animals you are allowed to eat:
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: any animal that both has a divided hoof and also chews the cud.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: However, if it either chews the cud, or has a divided hoof, then you may not eat it. These include: the camel, which though it chews the cud doesn't have a divided hoof, so it is unclean for you.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The rock hyrax, which though it chews the cud doesn't have a divided hoof, so it is unclean for you.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The hare, which though it chews the cud doesn't have a divided hoof, so it is unclean for you.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The pig, which though it has a divided hoof doesn't chew the cud, so it is unclean for you.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: You are not to eat their meat or touch their dead bodies. They are unclean for you.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: You are allowed to eat any creature with fins and scales that lives in the water, whether in the sea or in fresh water.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: But you are not allowed to eat any of the many creatures that don't have fins and scales that live in the water, whether in the sea or in fresh water.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They are repulsive.  You must not eat their meat, and you must treat their dead bodies as repulsive.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: All such water creatures that don't have fins and scales are to be repulsive to you.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: As for the birds,  these must not be eaten because they are repulsive: eagle, griffon vulture, bearded vulture,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: buzzard, kite and similar birds of prey,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: any raven or crow,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: tawny owl, long-eared owl, gulls, any kind of hawk,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: little owl, fish owl, eagle owl,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: barn owl, desert owl, Egyptian vulture,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: storks and any kind of heron, hoopoe, and bats.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: All flying insects that crawl are repulsive to you.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: But you can eat the following kinds of flying insects that crawl: those that have jointed legs they use to jump.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: So in this category you can eat any kind of locust, bald locust, cricket, or grasshopper.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: All other flying insects that crawl are repulsive to you,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: and will make you unclean. If you touch their dead bodies you will be unclean until the evening,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: and if you pick up one of their dead bodies you must wash your clothes, and you will be unclean until the evening.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Every animal with hooves that are not divided, or that does not chew the cud, is unclean for you. If you touch any of them you will be unclean.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Any four-legged animal that walks on its paws are unclean for you. If you touch their dead bodies you will be unclean until the evening,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: and if you pick up one of their dead bodies you must wash your clothes, and you will be unclean until the evening. They are unclean for you.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The following animals that run along the ground are unclean for you: rats, mice, any kind of large lizards,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: geckos, monitor lizards, wall lizards, skinks, and chameleons.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: These animals that run along the ground are unclean for you. If you touch a dead one of them you will be unclean until the evening.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Anything that one of them dies and lands on becomes unclean. Whatever it is—something made of wood, clothing, leather, sackcloth, or any work tool—it must be washed with water and will be unclean until the evening. Then it will become clean.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: If one of them falls into a clay pot, all that's in it becomes unclean. You must smash the pot.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: If water from that pot touches any food, that food becomes unclean, and any drink from a pot like that also becomes unclean.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Anything that one of their dead bodies falls on becomes unclean. If it's an oven or a stove,  it must be smashed. It is permanently unclean for you.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: On the other hand, if it's a spring or cistern containing water then it will remain clean, but if you touch one of these dead bodies in it you will be unclean.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Similarly, if one of their dead bodies falls on any seed used for sowing, the seed remains clean;
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: but if the seed has been soaked in water and one of their dead bodies falls on it, it is unclean for you.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: If an animal that you are allowed to eat dies, anyone who touches the dead body will be unclean until the evening.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: If you eat anything from the dead body you must wash your clothes and you will be unclean until the evening. If you pick up the dead body you must wash your clothes and you will be unclean until the evening.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Every animal that crawls along the ground is repulsive—you must not eat it.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Don't eat any animal that crawls along the ground, whether it moves on its belly or walks on four feet or many feet. All such animals are repulsive.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Don't defile yourselves by any such crawling animal. Don't make yourselves unclean or defiled by them,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: because I am the Lord your God; so dedicate yourselves and be holy, because I am holy. Don't defile yourselves by any animal that crawls along the ground.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: I am the Lord who led you out of Egypt so that I could be your God. So be holy, because I am holy.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: These are the regulations about animals, birds, everything that lives in the water, and all animals that crawl along the ground.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: You must recognize the difference between unclean and clean, between those animals that can be eaten and those that can't.
segments.speaker: Unknown
